bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am opening with a high-utility bid targeting my maximum possible utility. Since there is no evidence about the opponent’s priorities, I should start by anchoring on my top value for every issue and only concede later if their bids reveal tradeoffs.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s first offer is very low for my preferences, especially on high-importance issueE and issueB, and there are no messages indicating any reliable constraint. I will make only a tiny concession by moving issueD from valueE to valueD, which costs me very little while avoiding repeating my exact prior bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.87, since it is still very early at t=0.04 and there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers are very low for me and even worsened on issueE, so I will hold firm on my most important issues while conceding only on low-weight issueC, where they have repeatedly offered valueB.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and show no real concession pattern. I am therefore targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on a lower-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my last offer.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is little need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has repeated very low-value offers for me and has not shown meaningful concession, and there are no messages providing additional credible signals. I will make only a small concession on the lower-weight issueC while holding firm on the highest-weight issues, especially issueE, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.855 because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has not materially conceded; their offers remain very low for me and alternate only on issueE. I am making concessions mainly on lower-weight issues issueA and issueC, including issueC=valueB which the opponent repeatedly proposes, while holding firm on my most important issues issueE, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.85 because it is still very early at t=0.12 and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low for me with no real concession pattern. Their repeated bids suggest they may care about issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueC/valueB, and issueD/valueA, so I concede mainly on lower-cost areas like issueC and slightly on issueA/issueD while holding firm on my highest-value issueE and important issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the early-stage concession range, since time is only 0.14 and the opponent has not meaningfully improved their offers to me. Their repeated bids suggest they strongly prefer issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueC/valueB, issueD/valueA, and issueE/valueA or valueB, so I am conceding on my lower-weight issues A and C while holding firm on the most important issues B, D, and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.79 for me because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession, repeatedly offering very low-value combinations for my preferences. I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues issueA and issueC, while holding firm on the most important issueE and issueB, and making only a small concession on issueD from valueE to valueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.79 for me, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated essentially the same low-utility offers for me without meaningful concession, so I will keep my most important issues strong while conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueC, which they consistently request.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 while making concessions mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueC. The opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering very low-value bids for me, so I should hold firm on my most important issueE and important issueB while offering some movement on issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around 0.75, since it is early at t=0.22 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded from their low-utility offers. Their repeated bids suggest they strongly prefer issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and low issueE, so I am conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueC and making a moderate concession on issueB/D while holding firm on the most important issueE.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, since it is still early enough to avoid major concessions but worth showing movement on lower-weight issues. The opponent has not meaningfully conceded and keeps repeating very low-utility offers for me, so I am holding firm on the most important issueE while conceding issueA and issueC to values they repeatedly propose.

I am targeting a utility around 0.75, keeping firm on the most important issueE while making concessions on lower-cost issues like issueA and issueC. At t=0.26 there is still time, and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and issueE alternating valueA/valueB. I will slightly vary from my last bid by conceding issueD from valueE to valueD, which costs me very little while signaling flexibility.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68 by keeping my highest-weight issueE at valueE and issueB at valueE, while conceding on issueA, issueC, and now issueD toward the opponent’s repeated pattern. At t=0.28 there is some room to concede but it is still too early to drop close to the opponent’s offers, which remain low for me and show no real concession trend.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a still-solid utility while making a meaningful concession because the opponent has held firm around the same low-utility bids for me. Their repeated offers suggest they care about issueA=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and possibly issueB=valueA / issueE=valueA, so I concede on lower/medium priority issues while holding my most important issueE at valueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.65 while making a visible concession from my last comparable offers, since we are only at t=0.32 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded. Their offers consistently favor issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and issueE=valueA/valueB, so I am conceding on issueA, issueC, and issueD, plus a small concession on high-weight issueE to valueD while holding firm on issueB.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.61: still well above the opponent’s very low offers to me, but a real concession from my earlier bids. The opponent has been almost completely fixed on issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and issueE=valueA/valueB, so I am conceding on several issues they consistently signal while preserving acceptable value on higher-weight issueE and issueB. Since time is only 0.34, I do not need to collapse to their offer yet.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.60 because we are still before the midpoint, so I should not drop to the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent has shown almost no concession, repeatedly offering issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueD/valueA, and only toggling issueE between valueA/valueB, so I am conceding mainly on already-lower-priority or repeatedly demanded areas while preserving value on the most important issueE. There are no opponent messages to weigh.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.49, conceding further because we are moving into the mid-game but still not at the deadline. The opponent has held very firm on issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA and only alternates issueE between valueA/valueB, so I am matching their apparent priorities on the lower-to-mid value issues while preserving some value on my highest-weight issueE.

I am targeting a moderate concession utility around 0.40, still well above my reservation value but responsive to the opponent’s repeated preference pattern. The opponent has held firm on issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and only varies issueE between valueA/valueB, so I will concede on all low-to-mid issues and move issueE down from valueD to valueC while still protecting my most important issue somewhat at t=0.40.

I am targeting about 0.40 utility, since we are still before the halfway point and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded beyond alternating issueE between valueA/valueB. Their repeated bids signal strong attachment to issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueC/valueB, and issueD/valueA, so I keep those while holding issueE at valueC rather than dropping to their preferred valueB this early.

I targeted a low but still positive utility bid around 0.31 because the opponent has held almost completely firm through t=0.44 and rejected my prior concessions down to issueE=valueC. Their only recurring concession signal is sometimes offering issueE=valueB instead of valueA, so I am matching their signaled acceptable structure while preserving slightly more value than their latest offer.
